steps.text: Hold a virtual public hearing on March 29, 2024.
Submit comments identified by Docket ID No. EPA–HQ–OAR–2022–0491.

preconditions.text: A request for a virtual public hearing is received on or before March 8, 2024.

constraints.text: All submissions received must include the Docket ID No. for this rulemaking.
Regulator should be constructed of the appropriate materials that minimize analyte adsorption and reactivity.

The calibration gas manifold must provide accurate dilution of the calibration gas as necessary, monitor calibration gas pressure, and introduce analyte spikes into the sample stream (prior to the particulate filter) at a precise and known flowrate.
A filter (required) must be connected at the outlet of the heated probe and rated for 99% removal efficiency of 1 micron (μm) aerodynamic particulate.
Tubing must be Polytetrafluoroethane (PTFE), 316-stainless steel, or other inert material, of suitable length and diameter used to connect cylinder regulators to the gas manifold.
Sample Line construction material should minimize adsorption of analytes and the length of line needed.
The Computer/Data Acquisition System must have enough data storage space to archive all necessary infrared and meta data.
The Gas Absorption Cell must have the ability to monitor the pressure and temperature of the sample gas.
The Sampling System must be validated as detailed in section 9.4.

steps.text: Verify the gas cell pathlength according to the procedures in revised section 10.6.4.
Perform the QA spike procedure using a certified standard or a non-certified standard.
Perform sampling procedures, including performing a stratification check.
Compare the pre- and post-test CTS spectra.
Record and report data according to the revised requirements.
Store digital data incorporating the requirements from section 8.4.

preconditions.text: The method must be validated for each of the target analytes at the source.

constraints.text: The CTS must be ethylene, methane, or carbon dioxide. Other compounds may be used only with administrator approval.

steps.text: Use a heated head pump that is constructed from materials non-reactive with the analytes of interest.

constraints.text: The calibration gas manifold must provide accurate dilution of the calibration gas as necessary, monitor calibration gas pressure, and introduce analyte spikes into the sample stream (prior to the particulate filter) at a precise and known flowrate.
Regulator should be constructed of the appropriate materials that minimize analyte adsorption and reaction with the regulator.
Tubing must be PTFE, 316-stainless steel, or other inert material, of suitable length and diameter used to connect cylinder regulators to the gas manifold.

steps.text: Optionally use an independent FTIR and sampling system to measure the native source concentration throughout each standard addition.
Perform pre-test spiking consisting of at least 3 replicates.
Perform post-test spiking consisting of at least 3 replicates.
Inject zero air into the system to begin determining response time (RT).
Measure the native stack concentration of the species to be spiked.
Determine the dilution factor (DF) for each dynamic spike via a tracer.
Calculate the Standard Addition Response (SAR).

preconditions.text: Ensure the concentration has stabilized, which occurs when variability appears constant for five minutes.
If the tracer is not native to the source emissions, use Equation 1 to determine DF.
If the tracer gas is native to the source emissions, use Equation 2 to determine DF.

constraints.text: Spike gas flow rate must not contribute more than 10% of the total volumetric flow rate through the FTIR.
If spiking is not performed after each run and the post-test spike fails, all data for that test are invalid.

steps.text: Use section 5.3 of Method 301 (40 CFR part 63, appendix A), the Analyte Spike procedure, with the specified modifications.
Introduce the spike gas at the back of the sample probe.
Use a single FTIR system (or more) to collect and analyze spectra.
Perform all of the validation measurements sequentially in a single 'run'.

preconditions.text: Show that the exhaust gas characteristics are similar if applying validation from one source to another type of source.

constraints.text: The analyte spike must be performed dynamically, meaning the spike flow is continuous and constant as spiked samples are measured.
Spiked effluent must be carried through all sampling components downstream of the probe.
The measurements analyzed statistically must each be independent.
A validation data set must consist of 12 or more spike replicates.

steps.text: Collect two background spectra in succession while nitrogen or zero air is continuously flowing through the cell.
Calculate NEARMSCTS per the equation.
Obtain reference spectra for each analyte, interferant, surrogate, CTS, and tracer.
Report traceability and other pertinent information for each reference spectrum, for each compound.
Report the FTIR spectral resolution, line position, cell pathlength, temperature, pressure, and apodization function.

preconditions.text: The same averaging time must be used for NEA determination as will be used for actual sample collection.
If commercially prepared, or other available reference libraries are used to quantify data.

constraints.text: Resolution, line position, and apodization function used for collection of sample spectra must be the same as those of the reference spectra used for quantitation.
Reference spectra for each target compound must bracket the concentration of that compound in the sample stream.

steps.text: Seal the probe end of the system by capping or plugging the end of the sample probe.
Start sampling pumps and operate them until the pressure stabilizes.
Observe/measure the flow through the vacuum-side of the sampling system.
Seal the probe end of the system as close to the probe opening as possible by capping or plugging the end of the sample probe.
Operate all vacuum pumps. Draw a vacuum on the sampling system and let the pressure on the system stabilize.
Turn off the sample pumps and seal the system under a vacuum of 250 mmHg greater than the source static pressure.
Record the absolute pressure and the system absolute temperature every 30 seconds for 5 minutes.

constraints.text: For bypass systems, where the sample flow rate through the vacuum side of the sample system is greater than the FTIR cell flow rate, the higher flow rate (bypass plus analyzer/FTIR flow rate) is used as the in-use flow rate when calculating acceptability of the leak level.
A flow of less than 0.5% of the system’s normal in-use flow rate is acceptable.
The leak rate must be equal to or less than 2.5 mmHg per minute.

steps.text: Use a non-certified cylinder for an analyte that has been validated at the source (QA Spike Option 2).
Either spike each analyte of interest or choose an appropriate surrogate.
Use a probe of appropriate length to measure the analyte of interest at each of 12 traverse points (for a circular stack) or nine points (for a rectangular stack).
Calculate the mean measured concentration for all sampling points (MNavg).
Calculate the percent stratification (St) of each traverse point.

preconditions.text: The spike recovery for Option 1, as calculated by Equation 5, is within 70–130%.
The spike recovery for Option 2, as calculated by Equation 5, is within 90–110%.

constraints.text: Surrogates are not allowed for formaldehyde, HCl, HF, NH3, and vinyl chloride.
The wavelength at which the surrogate is to be quantified must be reported and be within 100 wavenumbers of a wavenumber that will be used to quantify the analyte of interest.
The pKa of a surrogate must be within 20% of the pKa of the analyte of interest.
Sampling must be done using a continuous flow of source gas.

steps.text: Keep the spike flow off when not in use.
Vent small volumes of laboratory gas standards through a laboratory hood.
Pack and dispose of neat samples.
Return surplus materials to supplier for disposal.

constraints.text: Spiked pollutants are vented with the extracted sample gas; minimize emissions.
Disposal must be according to applicable regulations.
